fields.label: Reading
fields.value: I Came Upon a Lighthouse — partially read

fields.label: Status
fields.value: None yet obtained — all in planning

lead:
This page documents my planned learning — certifications I haven't yet obtained, tools I haven't yet mastered, and courses I'm currently in progress on. **Nothing here is a current skill or finished item.** This is my honest future agenda.

My overarching career direction is **Computer Networking, Cloud Computing, and Cybersecurity**, and my certification plan reflects this directly. I've sequenced it: networking fundamentals first (CompTIA Network+, CCNA), then security (Security+, CEH), then cloud (AWS, OCI, Azure).

content.content:
**CS50 Introduction to Cybersecurity (Harvard)** — I started this on edX / cs50.harvard.edu. It covers cybersecurity fundamentals: threats, vulnerabilities, defensive techniques, cryptography basics, web security. Status: started, not yet completed. I still have remaining modules and the final project to do.

**Vim / Neovim** — Not fully started yet. Learning through vimtutor (built into Vim). Neovim with LazyVim config is already my editor in the [[linux-setup|Omarchy setup]], but deeper proficiency at the keybinding level is still being developed.

content.title: Currently reading
content.content: Partially reading 'I Came Upon a Lighthouse' by Shantanu Naidu — a personal memoir about an intergenerational friendship and mentorship. Not a business book. About human connection and life lessons. I need to finish the remaining chapters.

content.title: No industry certifications obtained yet
content.content: As of June 2026, I have no formal industry certifications. The Linux Fundamentals course certificate is a basic course cert. CS50 Cybersecurity is started but not completed. Everything below is planned with no set timeline.

rows.Why I want it: Good foundation before Security+. Networking fundamentals.
Gold standard for networking roles in India. High priority for me.
Most recognised first cert in cybersecurity. I'll do this after Network+.
EC-Council — offensive security focus. After Security+.
Easiest entry into cloud certs — validates cloud fundamentals.
Oracle Cloud — directly relevant given my existing [[homelab|OCI homelab]] setup.
Microsoft Azure entry cert — rounds out multi-cloud exposure.

content.content:
**Obsidian (advanced features)** — I currently use Obsidian as a personal wiki host but only scratch the surface. Features I want to actually learn: Dataview plugin (query notes like a database), Canvas (visual mind maps), graph filters, templates, tag hierarchies, backlink navigation, and core plugin configuration.

**Hermes Agent (Nous Research)** — A self-improving autonomous AI agent (Feb 2026, 123,000+ GitHub stars, MIT license). Runs across 15+ platforms from a single install. Natural language cron jobs, model-agnostic, closed learning loop, GEPA self-improvement. Status: on hold indefinitely. My current [[homelab|Oracle Cloud VM]] (1 OCPU, 1 GB RAM) is too limited — Hermes needs 24 GB+ RAM. Blocked until the ARM Ampere situation resolves.

I prioritise **CCNA and Security+** as the two highest-value items for my target career in networking and cloud. Both are recognised hiring signals in India — CCNA for network engineering roles and Security+ for security-adjacent roles.

My approach throughout is to build real things alongside studying theory. Each certification target connects to something I've already built: my [[privacy-stack|Privacy Stack]] demonstrates the practical networking knowledge that Network+ covers in theory; my [[homelab|homelab architecture]] demonstrates the cloud concepts that AWS/OCI certs formalise.

No timeline is set for any certification. The honest status is: none started, all planned.
None of these are current skills or finished. These are honest future agenda items.
content.author: Learning Plans document, April 2026

editHistory.summary: Added CS50 status, Hermes constraint note, cert sequence clarified
